name: FOLR1 autoantibodies / cerebral folate deficiency
description: Maternal or postnatal autoantibodies to FOLR1 block folate transport across blood-brain barrier; causes cerebral folate deficiency phenotype responsive to high-dose folinic acid.

name: Prenatal acetaminophen (paracetamol) exposure
description: Prenatal acetaminophen associated with increased autism/ADHD risk in multiple cohorts; biological plausibility via oxidative stress and endocannabinoid disruption.

name: Maternal folate deficiency during preconception/pregnancy
description: Inadequate methylated folate during neural tube formation increases autism risk; methylated folate supplementation reduces risk in MoBa cohort.

name: PFAS / forever-chemical drinking-water exposure
description: Endocrine-disrupting forever chemicals interfere with thyroid hormone, immune development, and neurodevelopment in utero; bioaccumulate over years; documented contamination in Miami-Dade and other airport-adjacent areas.

name: Glyphosate exposure (food + water)
description: Glyphosate disrupts gut microbiome shikimate pathway; secondary effects on neurotransmitter synthesis and immune development; epidemiology mixed but mechanistically plausible.

name: Mitochondrial dysfunction (acquired or inherited)
description: Subset of autism cases show elevated lactate/pyruvate, abnormal muscle biopsy, and respond to mitochondrial cocktails; may be primary or secondary to oxidative stress.

name: Gut microbiome dysbiosis / dysbiotic fermentation
description: Distinct gut microbiome composition in autism; enriched Clostridia and depleted SCFA producers; microbial metabolites cross BBB and modulate behavior; FMT trials show durable improvement.

name: Maternal immune activation (prenatal infection or autoimmune)
description: Maternal infection or autoimmune activation during pregnancy releases cytokines that cross placenta and disrupt fetal neurodevelopment; replicated in animal models and epidemiological studies.

name: Advanced parental age (especially paternal)
description: Paternal age associated with de novo mutations including in autism-risk genes; effect size modest but consistent across cohorts.

name: Prenatal valproate (sodium valproate) exposure
description: Valproate during pregnancy causes ~10x increased autism risk; HDAC inhibitor mechanism; FDA warning; widely used as ASD model in animal research.
affected_population: Children of women on valproate during pregnancy

name: Phthalate exposure (prenatal+postnatal)
description: Phthalates endocrine disruptors; prenatal exposure neurodevelopmental delays.

name: BPA / bisphenol exposure
description: Disrupts thyroid+estrogen signaling; prenatal-autism associations.

name: Air pollution (PM2.5, traffic)
description: Strong epi evidence linking prenatal/early-life PM2.5 to autism.

name: Pesticide exposure (organophosphates, glyphosate)
description: Prenatal organophosphate documented; glyphosate increasing evidence.

name: Heavy metal exposure (Pb, Hg, Al)
description: Lead well-established; mercury/aluminum in autism debated.

name: Mold / mycotoxin exposure
description: Mycotoxins linked to immune dysregulation.

name: EMF exposure (controversial)
description: Postulated via oxidative stress; evidence sparse.

name: Maternal diabetes (GDM, T2)
description: Significantly increases offspring autism risk via inflammation/hyperglycemia.

name: Maternal obesity
description: Associated with offspring autism; partial inflammation mediation.

name: Maternal vitamin D deficiency
description: Maternal vitamin D deficiency in pregnancy associated with offspring autism.

name: Maternal omega-3 deficiency
description: Inadequate maternal DHA associated with neurodevelopmental risk.

name: Early-life antibiotics (especially first year)
description: Multiple early-life antibiotics disrupt gut microbiome maturation.

name: Cesarean section delivery
description: Bypasses vaginal microbiome seeding; modest risk increase.

name: Maternal SSRI in pregnancy
description: Modest risk increase; confounded by underlying depression.

name: Prenatal viral infection (rubella, CMV, flu)
description: Maternal immune activation; rubella historically; CMV ongoing.

name: PANDAS/PANS triggers (strep, mycoplasma)
description: Post-infectious autoimmune syndrome with sudden regression.

name: De novo mutations in synaptic genes
description: De novo LoF in SHANK3/NRXN/NLGN; whole-exome standard.

name: Inherited polygenic risk
description: Common variant polygenic risk explains ~50% of heritability.

name: Tuberous sclerosis (TSC1/TSC2)
description: Single-gene mTOR pathway; rapamycin FDA-approved.

name: PTEN hamartoma syndrome
description: Single-gene mTOR; macrocephaly+autism phenotype.

name: AMPK pathway dysregulation
description: Reduced AMPK activity correlates with impaired autophagy, mitochondrial dysfunction, and metabolic inflexibility in autism subset. Targetable by metformin, berberine, exercise, caloric restriction.
affected_population: Subset with mitochondrial markers + metabolic dysregulation

name: IGF-1 axis insufficiency
description: Low circulating IGF-1 reported in autism subsets, especially Rett (MECP2) and Phelan-McDermid (SHANK3). IGF-1 supplementation trials show signal in syndromic forms.

name: SIRTUIN / NAD+ depletion
description: Reduced NAD+ levels and SIRT1/3 activity in autism cohorts; implicated in mitochondrial dysfunction and synaptic plasticity deficits. Restoration via NAD+ precursors (NMN, NR) explored.

name: Oxytocin system dysregulation
description: Reduced plasma and CSF oxytocin in autism; hypermethylation of OXTR gene reported. Intranasal oxytocin trials positive in subset for social cognition.

name: Maternal/prenatal thyroid hypofunction
description: Maternal hypothyroxinemia during pregnancy associated with increased autism risk in offspring; iodine deficiency a major modifiable cause. Window: first trimester.

name: Maternal psychological stress (prenatal)
description: Severe maternal psychological stress during pregnancy associated with increased autism risk; mechanism via cortisol transfer and HPA programming.

name: Childhood/family emotional stress
description: Adverse childhood experiences and chronic family stress exacerbate autism phenotype severity (regression risk, behavioral dysregulation). Distinct from causation.

name: Excessive screen / smartphone exposure (early life)
description: Heavy screen exposure in 0-3y associated with autism-like social-communication delays; mechanism debated (displacement of social input vs direct neurodevelopmental).
affected_population: Children with high (>2h/day) toddler screen time

name: Sleep disruption / circadian misalignment
description: Sleep architecture abnormalities (reduced REM, fragmented sleep) common in autism; bidirectional with neuroinflammation and behavioral severity.
affected_population: ~50-80% of autism cases have sleep disturbance

name: Birth complications (hypoxia, instrumental delivery)
description: Perinatal hypoxia, forceps/vacuum delivery, and prolonged labor associated with modestly increased autism risk via ischemic injury and inflammation.

name: Preterm birth / NICU exposure
description: Preterm infants (<32 weeks) have 2-4x autism risk; mechanisms include white matter injury, NICU environmental stress, impaired microbiome seeding.

name: Ultra-processed food / Western diet pattern
description: Maternal and childhood diets high in ultra-processed foods associated with autism-like behaviors; mechanisms include additive exposure, fiber depletion, metabolic disruption.

name: Endocrine-disruptor exposure (personal care, cleaning)
description: Phthalates, parabens, BPA in personal care products and household cleaners disrupt thyroid and sex hormone signaling during critical neurodevelopmental windows.
notes: Overlaps with HYP-0011 (phthalates) but broader exposure context.

name: Childhood vaccine exposure (contested)
description: Repeatedly tested in large cohort and meta-analytic studies; no causal link established at population level. Subset claims of regression after vaccination remain contested. Per spec §9.1, this is a contested hypothesis with overwhelmingly negative epidemiology and persistent anecdotal claims.

name: Vitamin D deficiency (maternal + child)
description: Low maternal 25-OH-D associated with autism risk; supplementation in deficient children improves behavior. Modifies immune and calcium signaling.

name: Zinc deficiency / copper:zinc imbalance
description: Elevated copper:zinc ratio reported in autism; zinc cofactor for metallothioneins and 300+ enzymes. Supplementation normalizes ratio in subset.

name: Magnesium deficiency
description: Low RBC magnesium reported in autism; cofactor for NMDA regulation, ATP synthesis, GABA function. Common in Western diet patterns.

name: Iodine deficiency / thyroid hypofunction
description: Maternal iodine insufficiency during pregnancy disrupts fetal thyroid hormone, critical for neurodevelopment.
notes: WHO classifies iodine deficiency as preventable cause of mental impairment.

name: Selenium deficiency
description: Cofactor for glutathione peroxidase (antioxidant), thyroid hormone deiodination. Low selenium status linked to oxidative stress phenotype.
affected_population: Region-dependent (Brazil nut belt vs. selenium-poor soils)

name: Choline insufficiency (postnatal)
description: Methyl-donor and acetylcholine precursor; postnatal choline supplementation explored for cognitive benefit beyond prenatal.

name: Maternal autoimmune comorbidity
description: Maternal Hashimoto's, lupus, type 1 diabetes, and other autoimmune conditions associated with offspring autism risk; mechanism via maternal autoantibodies and chronic inflammation.
affected_population: ~5-15% of autism cases linked to maternal autoimmunity

name: Iron metabolism dysregulation
description: Iron deficiency anemia and ferritin abnormalities in autism subsets; iron critical for myelin, dopamine synthesis. Repletion shows behavioral benefit.

name: AGE / acrylamide cooking exposures
description: High-heat cooking (frying, charring) generates advanced glycation end products and acrylamide; chronic exposure linked to neuroinflammation. Modifiable via cooking methods.

name: Acetaminophen postnatal use (contested)
description: Postnatal acetaminophen exposure during fever/illness explored as potential risk factor; mechanism via glutathione depletion. Distinct from HYP-0002 prenatal acetaminophen.

name: Endocannabinoid system dysregulation
description: Reduced anandamide and altered CB1/CB2 signaling reported in autism cohorts; may underlie social cognition deficits and GABA-glutamate imbalance.

name: Bifidobacterium depletion (autism-specific)
description: Multi-meta-analyses show consistent reduction in Bifidobacterium abundance (esp. B. longum, B. breve, B. infantis) in autism vs. neurotypical children. Bifidobacteria produce GABA, modulate immune tone, protect epithelial integrity. Hazan 2024 case documents Bifidobacterium 0% → 1.56% post-FMT with verbal emergence.

name: Clostridia overgrowth / dysbiotic fermentation
description: Elevated Clostridium species (esp. C. histolyticum group, C. perfringens) reported in autism cohorts. Clostridia produce p-cresol, 4-ethylphenyl sulfate, propionate at high levels — all linked to autism-like behavior in animal models.

name: Akkermansia / Faecalibacterium depletion
description: Reduced Akkermansia muciniphila and Faecalibacterium prausnitzii (major butyrate producers) reported in autism cohorts. Both protect mucin layer and produce SCFAs.

name: Intestinal barrier permeability ('leaky gut')
description: Elevated zonulin, lactulose-mannitol ratio, and serum LPS reported in autism. Permits microbial metabolite translocation and chronic immune activation. Distinct from microbiome composition itself.

name: Microbial p-cresol / 4-EPS metabolite excess
description: Elevated urinary and plasma p-cresol and 4-EPS in autism; mechanism via tyrosine metabolism by Clostridia. Inhibits catecholamine synthesis (Bermudez-Martin 2021); induces autism-like behavior in mice (Hsiao 2013).

name: Fungal dysbiosis / candida overgrowth
description: Elevated Candida (especially C. albicans) reported in autism GI subset; produces propionic acid, ammonia, biofilms. Anecdotal antifungal response common in functional/integrative protocols.
notes: Less well peer-reviewed; functional med tradition strong.

name: Brain serotonin synthesis dysregulation (TPH2/vitamin D)
description: Low brain serotonin paired with elevated peripheral serotonin (hyperserotonemia) is a long-documented autism finding. Patrick & Ames 2014 propose this is mediated by vitamin D regulation of TPH2 (brain) vs TPH1 (gut) — vitamin D deficiency could preferentially impair brain serotonin synthesis while leaving peripheral synthesis intact.
notes: Patrick & Ames 2014 FASEB J hypothesis paper.

name: Brain glutathione depletion
description: Reduced GSH/GSSG ratio reported in autism brain and plasma; compromises antioxidant capacity, methylation, mercury detoxification. Sulforaphane raises plasma + brain glutathione (Liu 2020). Distinct from general 'oxidative stress' — the specific antioxidant pool that's depleted.

name: DHA membrane phospholipid insufficiency
description: Inadequate phospholipid DHA in neuronal membranes during developmental windows compromises receptor function, synaptic plasticity, microglial regulation. Distinct from omega-3 deficiency in general — phospholipid form (krill, salmon roe) vs triglyceride form (most fish oil) determines brain uptake.

name: Heat shock response insufficiency / 'fever effect' subtype
description: Curran 2007 documented that ~80% of autistic children showed behavioral improvement during fever. Subsequent animal work showed whole-body hyperthermia improves behavior in C58/J and Shank3B- mice. Hypothesis: a subset of autism reflects insufficient baseline heat shock response that is transiently rescued by HSF1 activation (fever, sauna, exercise, sulforaphane).

name: Hepatitis B vaccine (neonatal birth-dose)
description: Day-of-birth hepatitis B vaccination has been hypothesized as a specific autism risk factor distinct from the broader childhood vaccine schedule, on the basis of (a) the neonatal developmental window, (b) aluminum adjuvant content (~250 mcg per dose), and (c) the small marginal risk-benefit ratio in non-endemic populations where maternal HBsAg is negative. Gallagher & Goodman 2010 NHIS analysis reported a 3x odds ratio in male neonates; IOM 2011, Verstraeten 2003 VSD, and Andersson 2025 Danish nationwide cohort (n=1.2M) found no causal association. Per spec §1.1 and §9.1, the hypothesis is recorded with status=contested and the engine scores from the evidence mix — currently tilted strongly toward the null per study size and design.
notes: Status=contested; Gallagher 2010 positive vs IOM 2011 + Verstraeten 2003 + Andersson 2025 null. Per ACIP Sep 2025 review, universal birth-dose recommendation is itself under policy review.

name: Aluminum adjuvant cumulative exposure (vaccines)
description: Cumulative aluminum from adjuvanted vaccines through the routine childhood schedule (typically 4-5 mg by age 2 in the US schedule) has been hypothesized as a neurodevelopmental risk factor distinct from any specific vaccine. Andersson 2025 nationwide Danish cohort (n=1,224,176) found no association between cumulative aluminum exposure in the first 2 years and 50 chronic conditions including autism (adjusted HR 0.93 per 1-mg increase). Distinct from heavy metal hypothesis (HYP-0015) — aluminum specifically and timing around vaccinations.
notes: Andersson 2025 JAMA Ann Intern Med null at population scale; minority claims (Tomljenovic + Shaw, Exley) maintain concerns. Status=contested.

name: MMR vaccine specifically (contested)
description: Measles-mumps-rubella combination vaccine has been the most intensively studied vaccine-autism question. Wakefield 1998 Lancet (later retracted) initiated the hypothesis. Subsequent large cohort studies — Madsen 2002 (NEJM, Denmark, n=537,303), Jain 2015 (JAMA, US sibling design, n=95,727), Hviid 2019 (Annals Intern Med, Denmark, n=657,461) — found no association. Hooker 2014/2018 (Translational Neurodegeneration → retracted; republished J Am Phys Surg 2018) re-analyzed CDC DeStefano 2004 data and claimed an association in African-American boys vaccinated before 36 months. Status=contested per spec §9.1.
notes: Wakefield 1998 retracted (Lancet); 4 large cohort studies negative; Hooker 2014/2018 reanalysis claims subgroup effect.

name: Thimerosal exposure (contested, largely removed)
description: Thimerosal (ethylmercury preservative) was removed from essentially all US childhood vaccines by 2001 except some influenza formulations. Pre-2001 hypothesis: cumulative ethylmercury exposure → neurodevelopmental disorders. Post-2001: autism prevalence continued to rise despite removal, which the IOM 2004 review and subsequent studies cite as evidence against causation. Geier and Geier studies claimed association. Status=contested. Mainly historical given exposure removal.
affected_population: Pre-2001 US-born children; some flu vaccines still
notes: IOM 2004 review specifically rejected; mostly historical.

name: Lactate / pyruvate ratio elevation (bioenergetic biomarker)
description: Elevated blood/CSF lactate (≥22 mg/dL) and lactate:pyruvate ratio (>25) indicate anaerobic shift in cellular metabolism, reported across autism, schizophrenia, bipolar. Distinct from general 'mitochondrial dysfunction' hypothesis (HYP-0006) — this is the specific quantifiable bioenergetic biomarker. Magnesium (lactate clearance), B-complex (PDH/ETC cofactors), and CO2 (Bohr effect) are mechanistically targetable. anomalie_blue's Ray-Peat-influenced framing on X.
affected_population: Subset with elevated biomarkers; ~30-50% of mitochondrial subset
notes: Oh 2020 Korean cohort; bioenergetic framing emphasizes metabolism-first interpretation.
